perturbation.original_text: The Company has been duly incorporated and is validly existing as a corporation under the laws of the State of Maryland and is in good standing with the State Department of Assessments and Taxation of Maryland, with the full corporate power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Company is duly qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a material adverse effect on the condition (financial or otherwise), results of operations, earnings, business, properties or prospects of the Transaction Entities and each of their respective Subsidiaries, taken as a whole (a "Material Adverse Effect"). The Operating Partnership has been duly formed and is validly existing as a limited partnership in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Operating Partnership is duly qualified to do business as a foreign organization in good standing in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a Material Adverse Effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company has been duly incorporated and is validly existing as a corporation under the laws of the State of Maryland and is in good standing with the State Department of Assessments and Taxation of Maryland, with the full corporate power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Company is only required to be qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in jurisdictions where it has a physical office space, and where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a material adverse effect on the condition (financial or otherwise), results of operations, earnings, business, properties or prospects of the Transaction Entities and each of their respective Subsidiaries, taken as a whole (a "Material Adverse Effect"). The Operating Partnership has been duly formed and is validly existing as a limited partnership in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Operating Partnership is required to be qualified to do business as a foreign organization in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a Material Adverse Effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by stating the Company is only required to be qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in jurisdictions where it has a physical office space. This creates an in-text contradiction with the statement 'the Company is duly qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification' because if the Company is only doing business where it has a physical office then the meaning of 'all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification' becomes uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company has been duly incorporated and is validly existing as a corporation under the laws of the State of Maryland and is in good standing with the State Department of Assessments and Taxation of Maryland, with the full corporate power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Company is only required to be qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in jurisdictions where it has a physical office space, and where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a material adverse effect on the condition (financial or otherwise), results of operations, earnings, business, properties or prospects of the Transaction Entities and each of their respective Subsidiaries, taken as a whole (a "Material Adverse Effect"). The Operating Partnership has been duly formed and is validly existing as a limited partnership in good standing under the laws of the State of Delaware, with power and authority to own its properties and conduct its business as described in the Registration Statement, the General Disclosure Package and the Prospectus and to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement to which it is a party; and the Operating Partnership is required to be qualified to do business as a foreign organization in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification, except where the failure to be so qualified would not, individually or in the aggregate, result in a Material Adverse Effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a limitation on where the Company is required to be qualified to do business ('only required to be qualified...where it has a physical office space'). This directly contradicts the original text's broader requirement ('duly qualified to do business as a foreign corporation in good standing in all other jurisdictions in which its ownership or lease of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification'). The altered text narrows the scope of required qualification, creating ambiguity and a potential conflict about which standard applies, fulfilling the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent's aggregate fee for its services hereunder will be an amount equal to 3.15% of the gross proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares sold to Purchasers that are not affiliates of the Agent (such fee payable by the Company at and subject to the consummation of Settlement). The Company, upon consultation with the Agent, may establish in the Company's sole discretion an aggregate amount of Shares to be sold in the offering contemplated hereby, which aggregate amount shall be reflected in the Prospectus. The Company acknowledges and agrees that (i) there can be no assurance that the Agent will be successful in selling the Offered Shares, and (ii) the Agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company or any other person or entity if it does not sell the Offered Shares for any reason other than a failure by the Agent to use its commercially reasonable efforts consistent with its normal trading and sales practices to sell such Offered Shares as required herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent's aggregate fee for its services hereunder will be an amount equal to 3.15% of the gross proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares sold to Purchasers that are not affiliates of the Agent (such fee payable by the Company at and subject to the consummation of Settlement). The Company, upon consultation with the Agent, may establish in the Company's sole discretion an aggregate amount of Shares to be sold in the offering contemplated hereby, which aggregate amount shall be reflected in the Prospectus. The Company acknowledges and agrees that (i) there can be no assurance that the Agent will be successful in selling the Offered Shares, and (ii) the Agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company or any other person or entity regardless of the amount of shares sold for any reason other than gross negligence.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that the agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company 'if it does not sell the Offered Shares for any reason other than a failure by the Agent to use its commercially reasonable efforts consistent with its normal trading and sales practices to sell such Offered Shares as required herein.' The revised text changes the clause to 'regardless of the amount of shares sold for any reason other than gross negligence.' Thus, the first text implies, that the agent can be held liable, when there is a proof that Agent did not use commercially reasonable efforts, to sell the offered shares; and the second text says otherwise, if agent performs gross negligence it can be held liable, but in no other occasion. This could create disputes about the level of effort required from the Agent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent's aggregate fee for its services hereunder will be an amount equal to 3.15% of the gross proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares sold to Purchasers that are not affiliates of the Agent (such fee payable by the Company at and subject to the consummation of Settlement). The Company, upon consultation with the Agent, may establish in the Company's sole discretion an aggregate amount of Shares to be sold in the offering contemplated hereby, which aggregate amount shall be reflected in the Prospectus. The Company acknowledges and agrees that (i) there can be no assurance that the Agent will be successful in selling the Offered Shares, and (ii) the Agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company or any other person or entity regardless of the amount of shares sold for any reason other than gross negligence.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Agent incurs no liability if it fails to sell shares as long as it uses commercially reasonable efforts. The changed text removes this 'commercially reasonable efforts' clause, stating the Agent incurs no liability 'regardless of the amount of shares sold for any reason other than gross negligence.' This creates a strong contradiction because the initial contract implies liability for lack of commercially reasonable effort, while the revised contract eliminates this liability, broadening the agent's protection, directly contradicting the initial implication. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as the term of liability is inconsistently defined.
